Question: A COE grants the right to own and use a vehicle for [BLANK] years.
Reason: COE gives the right to own and use a vehicle for 10 years.

Question: When a vehicle is deregistered before COE expiry the owner may receive a COE [BLANK].
Reason: COE rebate depends on unused COE period at deregistration.

Question: COE renewal is done by paying the [BLANK] instead of bidding again.
Reason: Renewal requires PQP payment instead of bidding.

Question: If COE is not renewed within 1 month after expiry the vehicle must be [BLANK].
Reason: The vehicle must be deregistered after the grace period.

Question: Road tax is a mandatory tax required to use a vehicle on [BLANK] roads.
Reason: Road tax is required to legally use a vehicle on public roads.

Question: Road tax is commonly renewed for [BLANK] or 12 months.
Reason: Standard renewal periods are 6 or 12 months.

Question: Outstanding [BLANK] can cause road tax renewal to fail.
Agents should NOT calculate [BLANK] for road tax issues — this is outside their scope.
All off-peak vehicle schemes share one feature: [BLANK] usage hours.
Reason: All off-peak schemes have restricted usage hours.

Question: To drive an off-peak car during restricted hours the user must purchase an [BLANK].
Reason: An e-Day Licence allows use during restricted hours.
